tracks.meaning: the fundamental level of a hierarchical system or the underlying foundation of software architecture
a substance that neutralizes acids, or a compound containing a metal and a non-metal in ionic bonding
a fortified camp or military installation serving as a center of operations
the starting point or line from which competitors begin a race or competition
the bottom or foundation of a body part, such as the base of the skull or spine
a permanent settlement or camp established in a remote location for research or military purposes
a place where supplies, equipment, or materials are kept and managed
to establish or found something on a particular foundation or location
a person's home or the place where someone regularly stays
the fundamental facts or legal grounds upon which a claim or argument rests
the principal amount of money on which interest is calculated
the lowest structural layer of a building or road that distributes weight to the ground
the bottom or support surface upon which type or plates are mounted
a reference standard or unit used as a starting point for calculations or comparisons
a nitrogenous organic compound that forms part of DNA and RNA molecules
a fundamental fact, principle, or reason that serves as the foundation for an activity or belief
